title: Threshold Treasury With Proposal Execution
summary: Implement a PDA-backed SPL token treasury controlled by threshold approvals and proposal execution.
businessLogic: A creator initializes a treasury configuration with a fixed owner set, threshold, and mint-specific vault.
Owners create transfer proposals for a specific recipient and amount.
Owners approve proposals, and any owner can execute a proposal once the threshold is met.

instructions.name: initialize
instructions.description: Create multisig state, validate the owner set and threshold, derive the treasury PDA, and create the token vault.

instructions.name: propose_transfer
instructions.description: Create a proposal PDA for a recipient and amount, and record the proposer's approval.

instructions.name: approve
instructions.description: Let an owner record one approval for an open proposal without double-counting.

instructions.name: execute
instructions.description: After threshold approval, release funds from the treasury vault to the intended recipient token account and close the proposal.

invariants: Only configured owners may create proposals, approve proposals, or execute transfers.
Threshold must be positive, must not exceed the owner count, and the owner set must not contain duplicates.
Approvals must not be double-counted for the same owner.
Executed transfers must preserve the configured mint and recipient binding stored in the proposal.
Successful execution must close the proposal so it cannot be replayed.
failureConditions: Allowing non-owners to approve or execute.
Allowing duplicate approvals to satisfy threshold.
Allowing payout to a token account not owned by the intended recipient.
Allowing execution before the threshold is actually met.
